title: 1.1: States that when unencrypted upload requests are made on a bucket with encryption requirements, the request is rejected.

1.2: Reports the logs observed in the server trace logs to show runtime execution sequence of an unencrypted upload on a bucket with encryption requirements (e.g.: "statusCode": 403 or "statusMsg": "Forbidden").
1.3: Shows log entries to demonstrate attempts to delete a locked object are rejected (e.g.: "<ERROR> Failed to remove `myminio/lock-test/protected.txt")
1.4: Reports the server trace log entries that demonstrate that a GET request succeeds when storage backend data is corrupted manually (e.g.: ""status": "success"").

1.5: Shows test output to demonstrate how session policy restrictions are enforced on temporary accounts (e.g.: PutObject blocked when only GetObject is allowed, or reports the vulnerability that temporary accounts can create new service accounts bypassing the session policy restrictions.).
1.6: Shows test output demonstrating that a low privilege user can escalate their privileges to consoleAdmin by modifying their user mappings, showing that there is a vulnerability.
1.7: Identifies the root cause of the user mappings modification (e.g.: the IAM importer does not verify the administrative permissions.).
